title: Draco cinnabari
taxonomy: Draco cinnabari is a species of tree that is endemic to the Socotra Archipelago in Yemen. It belongs to the family Capparaceae and is also known as the Dragon's Blood Tree. The species is classified as a deciduous tree, characterized by its unique umbrella-like crown and bright red sap. It is considered a vital part of the Socotra Archipelago's ecosystem and has been designated as a protected species. The classification of Draco cinnabari is further supported by its distinctive morphology and reproductive biology.
uses: Draco cinnabari, also known as the Dragon's Blood tree, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in the Comoros Islands and Madagascar.
